description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with a prominent relief carving on its front face, depicting two human figures beneath an arched structure supported by columns. The stone has a weathered, uneven surface with visible chipping and discoloration, especially along the sides and edges. The back of the block is flat and unadorned, suggesting it was designed to be set against another surface or structure. The top edge appears to have a slightly raised, linear section, possibly for alignment or structural integration. The coloration is predominantly a reddish-brown, with patches of lighter, whitish weathering. The overall form and decorative motif suggest it may have served as an architectural or commemorative element in a historical or urban setting.
description: A weathered, reddish-brown stone block with a carved relief of two figures beneath an arch on its front face, showing signs of age and surface wear.
description_view_0: The front view displays the detailed relief carving of two figures under an arch, with visible surface wear and chipping.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the block's thickness and the rough, irregular texture of the stone, with no visible carvings.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a flat, unadorned surface, highlighting the block's rectangular shape and uniformity.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the stone's depth and weathered, chipped surface without any decorative features.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or ancient architectural site
attribute_installation_method: mortared or set into a masonry wall
attribute_weathering_pattern: Significant surface chipping and discoloration, with lighter patches indicating exposure to the elements and age-related wear.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible relief carving of two figures beneath an arch on the front face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous wall or architectural frieze.
